channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Litter, 30th April
item.description:
Road needs to be cleared of litter and cleaned and swept
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70, Maylands Drive, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged guardrail, 30th April
item.description:
The metal railing alongside the pavement has been bent out of shape, with several vertical bars distorted and the structure no longer properly aligned. This may reduce its effectiveness as a safety barrier between pedestrians and the road, and could present a risk to those using the footpath.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Fearon Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox on the grass behind wall of st Michael’s church hall, 30th April
item.description:
Fox had a terrible odour with flies. Children play on the grass

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Wickham Lane, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti under the A102 flyover, 30th April
item.description:
Graffiti

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Horn Lane, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Apartment bins overflowing, 30th April
item.description:
4 Triangle Road’s bins are overflowing ever since the scaffolding went up and the door on the bin area stopped working. It’s become a dumping ground for a tonne of rubbish which seems to not be collected by the council anymore.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westgate Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Communal balconies unswept and unwashed for months, 30th April
item.description:
Our communal balconies have not been swept or washed for at least 7 months. Weeds are growing out of the dirt in some areas and drains are clogged with dirt. This is despite complaints to the RSO and actually requesting the cleaner to attend to this work. The place is so neglected. Please assist.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-30, Jamaica Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter outside 29 Kingsgate Estate, 30th April
item.description:
Rubbish left outside property that doesn't belong to the tenant. Please arrange removal, tenant has mobility difficulties and is blocking pathway.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tottenham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pile of clothes, 30th April
item.description:
Someone has left a pile of Clothes near the insect hotel

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Slip, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wicker chair on pavement, 30th April
item.description:
Chair on pavement
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 109, Marlow Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vulgar graffiti, 30th April
item.description:
As pictured

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Oaks, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish bags in street, 30th April
item.description:
Refuse all over street since morning

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Winston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped car, 30th April
item.description:
Dumped car in parking bay in sir Francis way blue Citroen Picasso full of rubbish no mot or tax was on a certain driveway but recently moved out and now the car is in a much needed resident space GJ13TNU
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-39, Sir Francis Way, Warley, Brentwood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge potholes which have been reported but no action taken, 30th April
item.description:
These potholes have been there for months now. Cars are continuing to swerve to avoid them. They are getting bigger so tyres will. be affected. Cyclists are at risk and could easily hit the potholes and be thrown into the path of an oncoming vehicle. One report said they had been fixed. This is not true. The potholes remain and the situation is becoming far more serious so when will Bromley take appropriate action?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 94, Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken overhead cables, 30th April
item.description:
Utility pole with overhead cables broken and hanging low/swinging, potential safety hazard. Outside 30 Lovelace Avenue

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lovelace Avenue, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned caravan, 30th April
item.description:
Caravan has no number plates, taking 3 parking spaces
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenbay Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Malvern road opposite post office, 30th April
item.description:
A dumped sofa partially blocking the pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malvern Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Parks)

item.title: On the grass at the junction of Stuart road and Malvern road, 30th April
item.description:
This fly tip of a sofa and old kitchen cabinets has been here for weeks and now people are adding to it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stuart Road, London

item.title: Sluice is blocked in main pond, 30th April
item.description:
The sluice is blocked in main pond by the railings. It is beginning to smell due to hot weather.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Street, Ramsden, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
